items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the parallel structure that sets up the comedic ‘connecting the dots’ between two thefts across eras. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The repetitive, banal politeness and self-introduction (“the Tsar”) reproduces the era-clash humor and Bunsha’s drunken, bureaucratic social ritual. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the authoritarian, performative ‘royal’ demand that is funny coming from Bunsha. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the elevated/archaic melancholy tone and the comic mismatch with the mundane Soviet setting; “old woman” keeps the insult that triggers the wife’s reaction. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Correctly makes “demons” the agent and keeps the panicked, superstitious misreading of technology. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Retains the mock-biblical flourish and the joke of attributing a mechanical event to religious miracle. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. A good idiomatic rendering that functions as a showman/MC cue and conveys the mass, chaotic invitation. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Captures the breezy criminal satisfaction and the ‘lucky drop-in’ vibe without overexplaining. No issues.

items.original: Икра черная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Crucially avoids calling it “eggplant caviar,” preventing confusion and preserving the Soviet-status-symbol inversion joke. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the comic rhythm of the inventory and the self-incriminating, escalating-count bragging. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism and the shared-absurdity punchline about everyone ending up ‘treated’ (psychiatric ward subtext). No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
